title: Conjecture 1 (You–Liu; Zhai–Shu–Hong)
statement_text: For any graph $G$ of order $n \geq 2$, the following holds: $$\lambda_n(G) - \lambda_2(G) \leq n - 1,$$ or equivalently $\lambda_2(G) + \lambda_2(\bar{G}) \geq 1$, with equality if and only if $G$ or $\bar{G}$ is isomorphic to the join of an isolated vertex and a disconnected graph of order $n - 1$.
context_text: The Laplacian spread $\lambda_n(G)-\lambda_2(G)$ of a graph is trivially at most $n$; the conjecture sharpens this to $n-1$. It has been verified for trees, unicyclic, bicyclic, tricyclic, cactus, quasi-tree graphs, graphs of diameter $\neq 3$, and bipartite/$K_3$-free graphs.
attributed_to: You and Liu; Zhai, Shu, and Hong
paper_contribution: Proves that $\max\{\lambda_2(G), \lambda_2(\bar{G})\} \geq \tfrac{2}{5}$, establishing the first unconditional positive constant lower bound for $\lambda_2(G)+\lambda_2(\bar{G})$ over all graphs of order $n \geq 2$.
paper_title: The Algebraic Connectivity of a Graph and its Complement

title: Closing Question
statement_text: What is the supremum of all real numbers $c$ such that for any graph $G$ of order at least $2$, $$\max\{\lambda_2(G), \lambda_2(\bar{G})\} \geq c\,?$$
context_text: The paper proves the supremum is at least $\tfrac{2}{5}$. The self-complementary path $P_4$ satisfies $\lambda_2(P_4) = 2 - \sqrt{2} < 0.5858$, giving an upper bound on the supremum. The exact value is left open.
paper_title: The Algebraic Connectivity of a Graph and its Complement
